cli.usage:
tmoes — GNU/Linux container management tool (modernized C++17 version)

Usage:
  tmoes enter interactive TUI menu
  tmoes [mode] [args...] guides the container to run based on position state machine analysis

Global shortcut command options:
  --lang <zh_CN|en_US> Manually override the internationalized display language
  --quiet Quiet mode (suppress DEBUG and other output)
  --no-color disables color terminal output effects
  --help, -h View this help

docker.readme_content:
service docker start || systemctl start docker — Start docker
systemctl enable docker — Set docker to start automatically at boot
---------------------------------
docker ps — List currently running containers
docker ps -a — List all containers
docker start NAME — Start container
docker stop NAME — Stop container
docker attach NAME — Connect container
docker exec -it NAME /bin/bash — Execute /bin/bash against the container

faq.a1:
Instructions for creating users in the deb system

For security reasons, some software is prohibited from running with root privileges. With great authority comes great responsibility. If the root user operates carelessly, the system may be damaged.

You can use the following command to create a new ordinary user
#Create a new user named moe
adduser moe
#The entered password is hidden. After the creation is completed according to the prompts, you can add the user to the sudo user group in this tool.

Afterwards, if you need to escalate privileges, just enter the sudo command
For example sudo apt update

-------------------

Instructions for switching users
You can enter su - or sudo su - or sudo -i to switch to the root user
You can also enter su - moe or sudo -iu moe to switch back to the moe user
If you need to start VNC as an ordinary user, please switch to the ordinary user first, and then enter startvnc

-------------------

The command to create a new user in the arch system is useradd -m miku
where miku is the username
Enter passwd miku to change the user's password
If you need to add it to the sudo user group, you can use the sudo user group management function that comes with this tool (the system management option located in the Secret Garden)

-------------------
faq.a10:
There are currently two simple solutions (っ °Д °)
The former provides a fast terminal emulator that runs directly on the framebuffer on your system; the latter modifies language variables.
───
Solution 1 fbterm: apt install fbterm && fbterm
If startup fails, please manually execute fbterm
───
Option 2 Modify $LANG: export LANG=C.UTF-8
faq.a11:
Do I need to set the hardware clock to the local time zone and enable NTP time synchronization?
timedatectl set-local-rtc 1 --adjust-system-clock
Synchronizing with Microsoft NTP time synchronization server...
ntpdate time.windows.com
timedatectl set-ntp true
If you want to close it,then enter timedatectl set-ntp false
Configuring automatic time synchronization service...
systemctl enable chrony || systemctl enable chronyd || rc-update add chrony
If you want to disable it,then enter systemctl disable chrony
chronyc sourcestats -v
faq.a11_confirm: Set hardware clock to local time zone and enable NTP time synchronization?
faq.a2:
In a chroot container (non-proot) running on Android termux, when executing ping 127.0.0.1 as root, you may encounter the problem of insufficient socket permissions and Permission denied.

The solution is as follows:
Method 1:
Enter newgrp aid_inet to switch to the aid_inet user group, which has networking permissions for the Android system.

Method 2:
Create a new ordinary user, then go to the system management option of the secret garden of this tool and add the user to the sudo user group.
When TMOE_CHROOT=true is detected, the user will be automatically added to the aid_inet and aid_net_raw user groups.
If your host is an Android system, you need to join these user groups.

---------------
If some programs cannot connect to the Internet, please add the relevant users to the aid_inet user group, such as usermod -a -G aid_inet mysql
faq.a3:
chown -Rv ${CURRENT_USER_NAME}:${CURRENT_USER_GROUP} ${HOME}/.config/xfce4
If you encounter other permission problems, you can also manually execute chown -Rv ${CURRENT_USER_NAME}:${CURRENT_USER_GROUP} ${HOME}
faq.a4:
The inability to connect to the setup service is related to dbus. Please select the vnc/x11 crash option in the FAQ.
The inability to monitor battery information is also related to dbus.
Please note that the proot container may not have permission to start dbus-daemon --system, please skip this question.
Only when the value of the TMOE_CHROOT variable is true, that is, you are using the chroot/docker container installed by tmoe-manager, dbus-daemon will start automatically.
If you use a non-tmoe-linux container, you need to start it manually.
The startup command is sudo dbus-daemon --system --fork
You can enter rm /var/run/dbus/pid to delete the pid file.
faq.a5: Press Enter to uninstall gvfs and udisks2
faq.a6:
If login crashes after version update, you can enter rm -rf ~/.config/tencent-qq/ and log in again.
Press the Enter key to automatically execute the above command
faq.a7:
VNC/X11 crashes are usually related to dbus.
Please check whether dbus-daemon has been started correctly.
Start command: dbus-launch --exit-with-session startvnc
Or: export DISPLAY=:1 && dbus-launch startvnc
faq.a8:
If it cannot be opened, please manually enter rm -rf ~/baidunetdisk
Press the Enter key to automatically execute rm -vf ~/baidunetdisk/baidunetdiskdata.db
faq.a9: Do you need to uninstall mlocate and catfish
